extraction.one_line_summary: JACOBSEN Fabio won Stage 8 by perfectly timing his sprint, sitting in until final 200m while DECEUNINCK teammates led out too early at 5km.
tactical_patterns: DECEUNINCK started leadout at 5km, got absorbed, then confidently re-initiated in final kilometer showing tactical maturity after early misjudgment.
PHILIPSEN Jasper contested intermediate sprint to extend points jersey lead, revealing willingness to expend energy before critical final sprint.
UAE controlled final 1.2km with MOLANO Juan Sebastian positioned fourth wheel, but TRENTINO Matteo never pulled through exposing weak leadout.
BURGOS-BH placed rider in every breakaway through 8 stages, demonstrating systematic sponsorship visibility strategy regardless of breakaway viability.

rider_signals.JAKOBSEN Fabio: positive: second stage win, sat third/fifth wheel entire final kilometer saving watts while DECEUNINCK absorbed early positioning mistake
rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: neutral: contested intermediate sprint for points jersey but second in final sprint suggests diminishing returns on early energy expenditure
rider_signals.MEEUS Jordi: positive: fourth place finish shows progression toward stage win capability on pure flat sprints
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: negative: finished outside top 20 confirming he requires gradient or technical finish for competitiveness, flat sprints not viable
rider_signals.MOLANO Juan Sebastián: negative: UAE had numerical advantage in final kilometer but MOLANO faded to fifth showing leadout dependency over raw speed
